activities.name: Crack of Dawn run on New Years Day
activities.description: Or not. Happy 2026!

activities.name: Down to Bradley’s Head and back
Opua to Paihia. The most beautiful coastal trail of my life?
activities.description: I know that’s a tall claim, and my memory isn’t what it was, but check out the photos.

activities.name: Sailing in the Bay of Islands
activities.description: Wonderful evening sail on the “Vigilant” with Captain Nat, and landing on the first island that Cook landed on in NZ.

activities.name: Road to Opua, then coastal track
activities.description: So fun to do another run with Grace. 250’ straight down to the water then 200’ straight back up at the end. Ooof!

activities.name: Mount Pauanui up and around
activities.description: With [strava://athletes/64115131/11]Grace Gedye [strava://athletes/17522471/11]Chris Gedye and Gil. Steep and pretty

activities.name: Pauanui and the pacific Pacific
activities.description: With Grace Gedye. Magellan named or misnamed this ocean, but never discovered New Zealand. Today the broad ocean is well named.

activities.name: Family Walk along the windy Taupo lakeside
activities.description: This lake is bigger than Tahoe, and just as blue. Amazing.

activities.name: Farewell New Zealand
activities.description: Ran into the Taupō Park Run. Several hundred people… very impressive. We need to join the Seattle Park Run sometime.

activities.name: Welcome home from Bean
activities.description: Very slow, very sniffy, neighborhood jog. Photo is one last memory from the NZ coast

activities.name: Full Drainage Five
activities.description:
Visiting the eastern and western edges of the Green Lake drainage, then descending via Dayton makes for a nice smooth 5-miler.

Needed to stop a few times perhaps due to overdressing on this warm winter morning. Should have worn a Tshirt!

activities.name: Zone 2 challenge: Max distance under 130 bpm before walking
activities.description: Today I made it about 2 miles before walking a minor hill. Let see what I can do on a perfectly flat course.

activities.name: Gamifying Z2 again
activities.description: Yesterday I jogged in zone 2 for 2 miles before I touched the upper bound of the Z2 heart rate. Today I did 3.3 miles under the same rules. One big goal would be to get to 6 and then improve the pace until I get back to 10 min/mile under these rules. Not sure if it’s doable, but it would imply much better aerobic capacity than I have now.

activities.name: Better name: Green Lake Drainage Transect
activities.description: 2nd run on this new, geographically interesting, course. Same moving time as Monday, but only one stop, so much better.

activities.name: Census of public access to North Lake Union with Alan D
activities.description: We did this a couple of years ago as part of his census of all beaches in Seattle. Beautiful morning!

activities.name: Frost Eagle 5-miler
activities.description: Cold, sunny, rocky, and rooty. Went with [strava://athletes/25960443/11]Anita Uppal and Rima (who came 2nd overall!). Great event!

activities.name: Frosty morning walk before Expect Delays Coffee
Easy with pickups?
activities.description: 30-60 second pickups were fun but nothing felt easy today, and my heart rate reflected that.

activities.name: Special K
activities.description: These are the streets that give Tangletown its tangles.  Must have arisen from an oddly  shaped plat when this area went from farmland and forest to houses. All 3 streets start with the letter K. Climbed back up hill on the two alleys between them

activities.name: 5 Kenwood Hills
activities.description: Not too bad, but not improving.

activities.name: Liberated by a mini bonk
activities.description: As I was climbing the gentle route up Phinney I felt that familiar feeling and gave myself permission to skip the top of the hill. Liberating.

activities.name: Frosty dog jog + 3 miles
activities.description: Needed to finish my weekly miles… was feeling a little low due to a head cold.

activities.name: Not enough puff
activities.description: I’m almost a year out from my heart surgery but am still very limited on the cardio-pulmonary side. Trying to run at a 9:30 pace on the flat still makes my heart rate climb so that I have to walk after less than 2 miles. Just want to be able to keep up with my friends’ easy pace of about 9. That may or may not ever happen.

activities.name: Gentle run in the gentle rain
activities.description: Run/walk after 2.5 miles. 30s pickups in final 3 miles
